mnemonics: Thresholding is like a BI-NARY Decision: Black or White, In or Out, No grayscale in between.
keyPoints: Thresholding partitions an image into foreground and background by comparing pixel intensity to a set value.
Global thresholding uses a single, fixed threshold value for the entire image.
The key challenge is selecting an appropriate threshold value that accurately separates objects from the background.
commonMistakes: Assuming a single global threshold (e.g., 128) will work for all images regardless of lighting conditions.
Applying thresholding to a color image without first converting it to grayscale.
Not considering the impact of noise, which can create holes in objects or background speckles after thresholding.
